title: Global Workforce Seasonal Visa jobs :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Global Workforce Seasonal Visa jobs
List of eligible jobs for the Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV), including codes and job requirements.

## Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) requirements
To apply for 1 of these jobs, you must:
- have the relevant skills, experience and any other requirements of the job offered
- have a job offer for a seasonal job, and
- have a job offer from an employer accredited to hire workers under the Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) with an available GWSV .
Global Workforce Seasonal Visa
You can apply for a standard Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) to work in 1 of these jobs if you will be employed outside the seasonal peak period.
Seasonal visa job durations and hiring months
There are additional requirements you and your employer will need to meet if you want to apply for an AEWV.
Accredited Employer Work Visa

### Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) jobs list
Requirements for the job (if any)
Agricultural and Horticultural Mobile Plant Operator
Agricultural Technician
Crane, Hoist or Lift Operator — Chairlift Operator
Flower Grower — Tulip Growers only
Eligibility is limited to jobs that are for: a seasonal peak running from approximately December to February (additional seasonal peaks may run between September and May), and large-scale, commercial tulip growers.
- a seasonal peak running from approximately December to February (additional seasonal peaks may run between September and May), and
- large-scale, commercial tulip growers.
Horse Trainer — Thoroughbred Yearling Preparer only
Eligibility is limited to jobs that: run from approximately August to March, and are employed by registered New Zealand thoroughbred breeders, as noted on the list of recognised breeding operations provided by the New Zealand Thoroughbred Breeders Association, and are for the purpose of training young horses for the New Zealand Bloodstock’s Karaka Yearling Sales.
- run from approximately August to March, and
- are employed by registered New Zealand thoroughbred breeders, as noted on the list of recognised breeding operations provided by the New Zealand Thoroughbred Breeders Association, and
- are for the purpose of training young horses for the New Zealand Bloodstock’s Karaka Yearling Sales.
Mobile Plant Operators NEC — Snow Groomer only
Mountain or Glacier Guide
Outdoor Adventure Instructor
Primary Products Inspectors NEC
Snow Sports Instructor
Stationary Plant Operators NEC — Snow Maker only
Technicians and Trades Workers NEC — Ski Technician and Surfboard Maker only
Whitewater Rafting Guide
Winery Senior Cellar Hand
